content.full_text:
May 8, 1990

Keith A. Davis
Huckaby and Associates
228 So. Washington Street, Suite 201
Alexandria, VA 22314

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-289

Dear Mr. Davis:

We have received your letter of April 18, 1990, seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided to you regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.

In the circumstances described in your letter, a major donor committee made a contribution to a candidate-controlled recipient committee. The major donor reported making the contribution to this committee. The recipient committee subsequently notified the major donor that the contribution had been deposited into an account held by another committee controlled by the same candidate. I advised you that the major donor is not required to amend its campaign statement to reflect that its contribution was deposited by the candidate in another of his or her controlled committee’s accounts.

Your letter does not indicate that you are seeking advice on behalf of a person who has duties under the Act. Accordingly, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).2

A copy of your letter is also enclosed for your convenience.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.

Keith A. Davis
Page Two

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
